stimulus:
The following text is adapted from Willa Cather's 1918 novel My Ántonia.

The wagon trail wound down the slope, and at its lowest point the old wooden footbridge, borne by hefty stone piers, sturdy, as when first laid down to carry settlers across the swollen creek, stood half-submerged in the muddy water of the spring thaw.
stem: As used in the text, what does the word “borne” most nearly mean?
rationale: Choice D is the best answer because "held up" most nearly matches the physical sense of being supported by piers, which is the structural relationship the text describes. Choice A is incorrect because "endured" means tolerated suffering; piers do not endure a bridge in that sense. Choice B is incorrect because "carried away" means swept off; the bridge is still standing, half-submerged. Choice C is incorrect because "produced" means created; the piers do not create the bridge.
